facebook.texto: Okay I need to say something and I don't even care if people think I'm crazy for posting this at 2 in the morning. I worked at a farm supply store for 22 years. TWENTY TWO YEARS. And I watched something happen in that store that I cannot get out of my head, and it wasn't until my husband Bill almost died that I finally understood what I was actually seeing all those years. So here goes. You know what farmers never have to deal with? Their animals being eaten alive from the inside while the docto

analise_criativa.avatar: No real person visible in the creative image itself — the ad image shows a farm/rural building exterior with a truck parked outside, used as background context for a first-person confessional story hook
analise_criativa.editing: Static image of a farm supply store exterior paired with a long-form confessional text copy. Minimalist visual, relies entirely on the narrative hook in the ad copy. No graphic overlays or captions on the image.
analise_criativa.observation: The visual alone (farm building) would not pass a strict real-person filter, however the page avatar shows a woman silhouette and the hook copy is extremely strong — late-night confessional tone, parasitic framing ('animals being eaten alive from the inside'), salt trick angle. The image functions as a credibility prop rather than a scroll-stopper on its own. Approved on the strength of the narrative context and recognizable DR pattern.
analise_criativa.highlight: Exceptionally strong hook copy — uses curiosity gap, time-stamped authenticity ('2 in the morning'), personal stakes ('my husband Bill almost died'), and an unusual mechanism framing (farm animal observation). The 'salt trick' keyword combined with parasitic/internal health angle is a high-performing DR pattern worth testing with a real UGC face-to-camera version.

facebook.texto: GLP-1 has become one of the biggest wellness trends in recent years 💭 Why? Because GLP-1 is a natural hormone involved in appetite regulation, feelings of fullness, and metabolic function. But here's what many people don't realize: Your daily habits, diet, and gut health can all play a role in supporting your body's natural GLP-1 activity. That's why more people are exploring ways to support their wellness routines without injections. Bioma GLP-1 is formulated with science-backed ingredients des

analise_criativa.avatar: Woman in her 40s filming herself in a bathroom mirror with a man in the background, then two women talking in an elevator, then a dark-haired woman speaking to camera from inside a car
analise_criativa.editing: Vertical mobile-native video with casual, unpolished feel. Multiple scene cuts suggesting a testimonial or friend-recommendation narrative. Subtitles visible. Low production value consistent with organic UGC style.
analise_criativa.observation: Multiple characters across scenes may suggest a compilation or multi-testimonial format rather than a single narrator. The car talking-head scene at 30s is a classic DR hook format. Brand name Bioma is visible in subtitles which is borderline product exposure but not a physical product shown.
analise_criativa.highlight: The bathroom mirror selfie opening with a couple in frame is highly relatable and scroll-stopping for women 35-55. The car talking-head segment is a proven DR format. Multi-avatar approach could allow split testing individual scenes as standalone hooks.

facebook.texto: Psssst…. Anti-Spike isn’t just the best supplement to reduce your spikes and support your fasting glucose levels. It also increases your GLP-1 levels by up to 22% (yes, the same molecule as things like Ozempic act on). It’s an amazing natural combo of plant extracts. Comment your favorite ice cream flavor and I’ll send you the link ❤️ Natural supplement with clinically studied ingredients that reduces glucose spikes by up to 40%. Lower fasting glucose, reduce cravings & boost energy. Science-bac

analise_criativa.avatar: Young woman with short dark hair, casual black outfit, filmed in what appears to be a kitchen or home setting, holding and eating from a small cup of ice cream or dessert throughout the video
analise_criativa.editing: Vertical TikTok-style raw selfie video, minimal editing, no captions visible, natural lighting, very authentic and unpolished UGC aesthetic
analise_criativa.observation: The hook is clever — she uses an ice cream flavor comment mechanic to drive engagement and DM traffic, which is a common organic-to-paid bridge tactic. The product is never shown on screen, keeping the focus on the person and the curiosity gap. The GLP-1 claim tied to a fun food prop is unusual and attention-grabbing.
analise_criativa.highlight: Strong scroll-stop potential: real person eating ice cream while talking about a natural Ozempic alternative creates cognitive dissonance. The comment mechanic ('tell me your favorite ice cream flavor') is a high-engagement DR tactic worth testing. Format feels native and not like an ad, which increases trust.

facebook.texto: Psssst…. Anti-Spike isn’t just the best supplement to reduce your spikes and support your fasting glucose levels. It also increases your GLP-1 levels by up to 22% (yes, the same molecule as things like Ozempic act on). It’s an amazing natural combo of plant extracts. Lower fasting glucose, reduce cravings & boost energy. Science-bac

analise_criativa.avatar: Young woman with short dark hair holding what appears to be a food/dessert product, filmed in a casual home setting — likely a kitchen or living room. She speaks directly to camera in a conversational, relatable tone.
analise_criativa.editing: Vertical mobile-shot video, no heavy editing, natural lighting, raw UGC aesthetic typical of TikTok-style content. Low production value enhances authenticity.
analise_criativa.observation: The ad explicitly names the product 'Anti-Spike' and references Ozempic by name, which creates brand/product exposure that could conflict with the DR model of curiosity-first. The comment-bait mechanic ('comment your favorite ice cream flavor') is a common engagement hack. The product packaging is visible in the creator's hand across frames, which borderlines on product display — however the main character (real person) dominates the frame and the visual is not product-centric.
analise_criativa.highlight: The Ozempic angle is highly relevant given the keyword 'natural ozempic' — strong keyword-creative alignment. The ice cream flavor comment mechanic is an effective engagement trigger. UGC format with a real relatable woman creates trust. The GLP-1 / natural Ozempic hook is a proven high-curiosity angle in current market.

repeated_creatives.description: Identical opening text ('Psssst…. Anti-Spike isn't just the best supplement...'), same young woman with short dark hair in a casual home setting, same product and messaging — likely the same source video repurposed or minimally edited across two ad runs

frequent_avatars.type: Everyday woman in her 30s–40s filming herself in a relatable home or car setting, speaking directly to camera in a confessional or conversational tone
Anonymous first-person narrator with no face shown — story told through text overlay or voiceover against a lifestyle/environmental background

facebook_cross_analysis.dominant_mechanism: GLP-1 hormone activation as a natural, drug-free alternative to Ozempic for appetite suppression and metabolic regulation
facebook_cross_analysis.emerging_angle: Farm/rural supply worker as an unlikely credibility source — a 22-year blue-collar insider revealing an overlooked 'salt trick' weight loss secret, leveraging working-class authenticity and pattern-interrupt curiosity at an unusual posting hour (2am) to drive engagement
facebook_cross_analysis.market_signal: Both UK and US markets are heavily testing 'natural Ozempic' positioning that borrows GLP-1 drug credibility without prescription associations, using UGC confessional formats and relatable female avatars to lower skepticism around supplement efficacy
